seoTitle: How to Create Multiple Workspaces in Trello
metaDescription: Set up additional Trello Workspaces to separate client accounts, businesses, or departments, with distinct permissions and privacy settings.
intro: One Trello Workspace can get cluttered fast if you're juggling multiple clients, businesses, or departments. This guide covers spinning up a second Workspace with its own access permissions and privacy settings so your boards stay organized.

steps.heading: Find the creation panel
steps.body: From your account header, locate the command pathway for spawning a new workspace.

steps.heading: Choose a workspace type
steps.body: Select a functional industry field that best describes what this workspace will be used for.

steps.heading: Write a workspace description
steps.body: Add relevant context in the public description box so members understand its purpose.

steps.heading: Set your privacy level
steps.body: Choose between public, workspace-visible, or fully locked enterprise privacy settings depending on your needs.

steps.heading: Invite collaborators
steps.body: Generate a shareable onboarding link or enter team email addresses to add members.

steps.heading: Skip invites if you're not ready
steps.body: Use alternative commands to build and polish your workspace privately before inviting anyone.

faq.question: Why would I need more than one Trello Workspace?
faq.answer: Separate workspaces are useful for isolating client accounts, distinct businesses, or internal departments that shouldn't share visibility.

faq.question: Can I control who sees each workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose between public, workspace-visible, or fully locked privacy configurations for each workspace.

faq.question: Do I have to invite members right away?
faq.answer: No, you can bypass the invite step and build out your workspace privately before adding collaborators.

faq.question: Can I brand each workspace differently?
faq.answer: Yes, custom branding logos can help distributed teams quickly identify which workspace they're working in.
